articles.title: Big News: Justice Department Drops Investigation Into Fed Chair
articles.summary: The Justice Department has stopped looking into whether Fed Chair Jerome Powell lied about building renovations. This means Kevin Warsh might become the next Fed chair. A court also said President Trump can't ban asylum at the border. And the FDA will fast-track three psychedelic drugs for mental health. A tornado hit Oklahoma, destroying homes but no one died. Wildfires in Georgia forced more evacuations.

articles.title: Pittsburgh Hosts NFL Draft for First Time in Almost 80 Years!
articles.summary: Pittsburgh is hosting the NFL draft for the first time since 1947! Up to 700,000 fans are expected. The city is a sports mecca with 16 major championships. The Steelers' 1970s "Steel Curtain" defense won four Super Bowls. The Pirates and Penguins also have multiple titles. Locals say the city's blue-collar work ethic drives its winning spirit.

articles.title: Colombia's President Visits Venezuela After Maduro's Abduction
articles.summary: Colombian President Gustavo Petro visited Venezuela, becoming the first foreign leader to do so since the US military abducted former President Nicolas Maduro in January. Petro met interim President Delcy Rodriguez. They discussed border security, trade, and migration. Petro has criticized the US abduction. Rodriguez has cooperated with some US demands while trying to attract investors to fix Venezuela's economy.
